quotes.text: The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.
quotes.author: Chinese Proverb

quotes.text: What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us.
quotes.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quotes.text: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
quotes.author: Steve Jobs

quotes.text: It is during our darkest moments that we must focus to see the light.
quotes.author: Aristotle

quotes.text: Believe you can and you're halfway there.
quotes.author: Theodore Roosevelt

quotes.text: Act as if what you do makes a difference. It does.
quotes.author: William James

quotes.text: Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts.
quotes.author: Winston Churchill

quotes.text: The future belongs to those who believe in the beauty of their dreams.
quotes.author: Eleanor Roosevelt

quotes.text: You are never too old to set another goal or to dream a new dream.
quotes.author: C.S. Lewis

quotes.text: In the middle of every difficulty lies opportunity.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: Start where you are. Use what you have. Do what you can.
quotes.author: Arthur Ashe

quotes.text: Everything you've ever wanted is on the other side of fear.
quotes.author: George Addair

quotes.text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
quotes.author: Mark Twain

quotes.text: It always seems impossible until it's done.
quotes.author: Nelson Mandela

quotes.text: Keep your face always toward the sunshine and shadows will fall behind you.
quotes.author: Walt Whitman

quotes.text: What you get by achieving your goals is not as important as what you become by achieving your goals.
quotes.author: Zig Ziglar

quotes.text: Don't watch the clock; do what it does. Keep going.
quotes.author: Sam Levenson

quotes.text: The only impossible journey is the one you never begin.
quotes.author: Tony Robbins

quotes.text: With the new day comes new strength and new thoughts.
quotes.author: Eleanor Roosevelt

quotes.text: Do small things with great love.
quotes.author: Mother Teresa

quotes.text: The unexamined life is not worth living.
quotes.author: Socrates

quotes.text: We suffer more in imagination than in reality.
quotes.author: Seneca

quotes.text: The obstacle is the way.
quotes.author: Marcus Aurelius

quotes.text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
quotes.author: Friedrich Nietzsche

quotes.text: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
quotes.author: Socrates

quotes.text: Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom.
quotes.author: Aristotle

quotes.text: The measure of intelligence is the ability to change.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste a great deal of it.
quotes.author: Seneca

quotes.text: No man is free who is not master of himself.
quotes.author: Epictetus

quotes.text: The mind is everything. What you think you become.
quotes.author: Buddha

quotes.text: Patience is bitter, but its fruit is sweet.
quotes.author: Aristotle

quotes.text: The journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
quotes.author: Lao Tzu

quotes.text: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence is not an act, but a habit.
quotes.author: Aristotle

quotes.text: To know what you know and what you do not know, that is true knowledge.
quotes.author: Confucius

quotes.text: The wise speak only of what they know.
quotes.author: J.R.R. Tolkien

quotes.text: Turn your wounds into wisdom.
quotes.author: Oprah Winfrey

quotes.text: Life is really simple, but we insist on making it complicated.
quotes.author: Confucius

quotes.text: When you know better, you do better.
quotes.author: Maya Angelou

quotes.text: The beginning of wisdom is the definition of terms.
quotes.author: Socrates

quotes.text: Still waters run deep.
quotes.author: English Proverb

quotes.text: In every walk with nature one receives far more than he seeks.
quotes.author: John Muir

quotes.text: The earth has music for those who listen.
quotes.author: William Shakespeare

quotes.text: Look deep into nature, and then you will understand everything better.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: The clearest way into the Universe is through a forest wilderness.
quotes.author: John Muir

quotes.text: Adopt the pace of nature: her secret is patience.
quotes.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quotes.text: Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished.
quotes.author: Lao Tzu

quotes.text: The ocean stirs the heart, inspires the imagination, and brings eternal joy to the soul.
quotes.author: Wyland

quotes.text: In all things of nature there is something of the marvelous.
quotes.author: Aristotle

quotes.text: Colors are the smiles of nature.
quotes.author: Leigh Hunt

quotes.text: The mountains are calling and I must go.
quotes.author: John Muir

quotes.text: To sit in the shade on a fine day and look upon verdure is the most perfect refreshment.
quotes.author: Jane Austen

quotes.text: Those who contemplate the beauty of the earth find reserves of strength that will endure as long as life lasts.
quotes.author: Rachel Carson

quotes.text: The poetry of earth is never dead.
quotes.author: John Keats

quotes.text: Sunshine is delicious, rain is refreshing, wind braces us up. There is no such thing as bad weather, only different kinds of good weather.
quotes.author: John Ruskin

quotes.text: Leave the road, take the trails.
quotes.author: Pythagoras

quotes.text: Spring is nature's way of saying let's party.
quotes.author: Robin Williams

quotes.text: Keep close to nature's heart and break clear away, once in a while, to climb a mountain or spend a week in the woods.
quotes.author: John Muir

quotes.text: A garden is a friend you can visit any time.
quotes.author: Japanese Proverb

quotes.text: Wilderness is not a luxury but a necessity of the human spirit.
quotes.author: Edward Abbey

quotes.text: Between every two pines is a doorway to a new world.
quotes.author: John Muir

quotes.text: I am so clever that sometimes I don't understand a single word of what I am saying.
quotes.author: Oscar Wilde

quotes.text: Be yourself; everyone else is already taken.
quotes.author: Oscar Wilde

quotes.text: I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work.
quotes.author: Thomas Edison

quotes.text: The only mystery in life is why the kamikaze pilots wore helmets.
quotes.author: Al McGuire

quotes.text: If you think you are too small to make a difference, try sleeping with a mosquito.
quotes.author: Dalai Lama

quotes.text: I find television very educating. Every time somebody turns on the set, I go into the other room and read a book.
quotes.author: Groucho Marx

quotes.text: A day without laughter is a day wasted.
quotes.author: Charlie Chaplin

quotes.text: Behind every great man is a woman rolling her eyes.
quotes.author: Jim Carrey

quotes.text: The best things in life are actually really expensive.
quotes.author: Oscar Wilde

quotes.text: I'm writing a book. I've got the page numbers done.
quotes.author: Steven Wright

quotes.text: People say nothing is impossible, but I do nothing every day.
quotes.author: A.A. Milne

quotes.text: Life is short. Smile while you still have teeth.
quotes.author: Mallory Hopkins

quotes.text: Age is of no importance unless you're a cheese.
quotes.author: Billie Burke

quotes.text: The road to success is always under construction.
quotes.author: Lily Tomlin

quotes.text: I used to think I was indecisive, but now I'm not so sure.
quotes.author: Tommy Cooper

quotes.text: Light travels faster than sound. That's why some people appear bright until you hear them speak.
quotes.author: Alan Dundes

quotes.text: If at first you don't succeed, then skydiving definitely isn't for you.
quotes.author: Steven Wright

quotes.text: You can't have everything. Where would you put it?
quotes.author: Steven Wright

quotes.text: The elevator to success is out of order. You'll have to use the stairs.
quotes.author: Joe Girard

quotes.text: Common sense is like deodorant. The people who need it most never use it.
quotes.author: Anonymous

quotes.text: Creativity is intelligence having fun.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: Every artist was first an amateur.
quotes.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quotes.text: Art is not what you see, but what you make others see.
quotes.author: Edgar Degas

quotes.text: The chief enemy of creativity is good sense.
quotes.author: Pablo Picasso

quotes.text: Creativity takes courage.
quotes.author: Henri Matisse

quotes.text: Every child is an artist. The problem is how to remain an artist once we grow up.
quotes.author: Pablo Picasso

quotes.text: The desire to create is one of the deepest yearnings of the human soul.
quotes.author: Dieter F. Uchtdorf

quotes.text: Imagination is the beginning of creation.
quotes.author: George Bernard Shaw

quotes.text: Art enables us to find ourselves and lose ourselves at the same time.
quotes.author: Thomas Merton

quotes.text: To practice any art, no matter how well or badly, is a way to make your soul grow.
quotes.author: Kurt Vonnegut

quotes.text: The earth without art is just eh.
quotes.author: Demetri Martin

quotes.text: Life beats down and crushes the soul, and art reminds you that you have one.
quotes.author: Stella Adler

quotes.text: The purpose of art is washing the dust of daily life off our souls.
quotes.author: Pablo Picasso

quotes.text: Great things are done by a series of small things brought together.
quotes.author: Vincent Van Gogh

quotes.text: Have no fear of perfection — you'll never reach it.
quotes.author: Salvador Dali

quotes.text: Creativity is allowing yourself to make mistakes. Art is knowing which ones to keep.
quotes.author: Scott Adams

quotes.text: The world always seems brighter when you've just made something that wasn't there before.
quotes.author: Neil Gaiman

quotes.text: You can't use up creativity. The more you use, the more you have.
quotes.author: Maya Angelou

quotes.text: Imagination is more important than knowledge.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: Art is the lie that enables us to realize the truth.
quotes.author: Pablo Picasso

quotes.text: The important thing is not to stop questioning. Curiosity has its own reason for existing.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: Somewhere, something incredible is waiting to be known.
quotes.author: Carl Sagan

quotes.text: We are all made of star stuff.
quotes.author: Carl Sagan

quotes.text: The good thing about science is that it's true whether or not you believe in it.
quotes.author: Neil deGrasse Tyson

quotes.text: Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood.
quotes.author: Marie Curie

quotes.text: Science is a way of thinking much more than it is a body of knowledge.
quotes.author: Carl Sagan

quotes.text: Two things are infinite: the universe and human stupidity; and I'm not sure about the universe.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: The most beautiful thing we can experience is the mysterious.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: Look up at the stars and not down at your feet.
quotes.author: Stephen Hawking

quotes.text: Equipped with his five senses, man explores the universe around him and calls the adventure Science.
quotes.author: Edwin Hubble

quotes.text: The saddest aspect of life right now is that science gathers knowledge faster than society gathers wisdom.
quotes.author: Isaac Asimov

quotes.text: Research is what I'm doing when I don't know what I'm doing.
quotes.author: Wernher von Braun

quotes.text: The universe is under no obligation to make sense to you.
quotes.author: Neil deGrasse Tyson

quotes.text: An experiment is a question which science poses to Nature, and a measurement is the recording of Nature's answer.
quotes.author: Max Planck

quotes.text: If I have seen further it is by standing on the shoulders of giants.
quotes.author: Isaac Newton

quotes.text: The cosmos is within us. We are made of star-stuff. We are a way for the universe to know itself.
quotes.author: Carl Sagan

quotes.text: Science is not only a disciple of reason but also one of romance and passion.
quotes.author: Stephen Hawking

quotes.text: The more I learn, the more I realize how much I don't know.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: Extraordinary claims require extraordinary evidence.
quotes.author: Carl Sagan

quotes.text: The Earth is the cradle of humanity, but mankind cannot stay in the cradle forever.
quotes.author: Konstantin Tsiolkovsky

quotes.text: No act of kindness, no matter how small, is ever wasted.
quotes.author: Aesop

quotes.text: Be kind whenever possible. It is always possible.
quotes.author: Dalai Lama

quotes.text: A warm smile is the universal language of kindness.
quotes.author: William Arthur Ward

quotes.text: Kindness is a language which the deaf can hear and the blind can see.
quotes.author: Mark Twain

quotes.text: Carry out a random act of kindness, with no expectation of reward.
quotes.author: Princess Diana

quotes.text: In a world where you can be anything, be kind.
quotes.author: Jennifer Dukes Lee

quotes.text: The best way to find yourself is to lose yourself in the service of others.
quotes.author: Mahatma Gandhi

quotes.text: What wisdom can you find that is greater than kindness?
quotes.author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau

quotes.text: Remember there's no such thing as a small act of kindness. Every act creates a ripple with no logical end.
quotes.author: Scott Adams

quotes.text: One kind word can warm three winter months.
quotes.author: Japanese Proverb

quotes.text: Tenderness and kindness are not signs of weakness and despair, but manifestations of strength and resolution.
quotes.author: Kahlil Gibran

quotes.text: How beautiful a day can be when kindness touches it.
quotes.author: George Elliston

quotes.text: We rise by lifting others.
quotes.author: Robert Ingersoll

quotes.text: The simplest acts of kindness are by far more powerful than a thousand heads bowing in prayer.
quotes.author: Mahatma Gandhi

quotes.text: Too often we underestimate the power of a touch, a smile, a kind word, a listening ear.
quotes.author: Leo Buscaglia

quotes.text: Wherever there is a human being, there is an opportunity for kindness.
quotes.author: Seneca

quotes.text: You cannot do a kindness too soon, for you never know how soon it will be too late.
quotes.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quotes.text: Love and compassion are necessities, not luxuries. Without them, humanity cannot survive.
quotes.author: Dalai Lama

quotes.text: There is no exercise better for the heart than reaching down and lifting people up.
quotes.author: John Holmes

quotes.text: Kindness in words creates confidence. Kindness in thinking creates profoundness. Kindness in giving creates love.
quotes.author: Lao Tzu

quotes.text: A reader lives a thousand lives before he dies. The man who never reads lives only one.
quotes.author: George R.R. Martin

quotes.text: Not all those who wander are lost.
quotes.author: J.R.R. Tolkien

quotes.text: It is only with the heart that one can see rightly; what is essential is invisible to the eye.
quotes.author: Antoine de Saint-Exupery

quotes.text: All that is gold does not glitter.
quotes.author: J.R.R. Tolkien

quotes.text: It does not do to dwell on dreams and forget to live.
quotes.author: J.K. Rowling

quotes.text: So we beat on, boats against the current, borne back ceaselessly into the past.
quotes.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

quotes.text: Whatever our souls are made of, his and mine are the same.
quotes.author: Emily Bronte

quotes.text: There is no greater agony than bearing an untold story inside you.
quotes.author: Maya Angelou

quotes.text: It is a truth universally acknowledged, that happiness depends on being free.
quotes.author: Jane Austen

quotes.text: Tomorrow is a new day with no mistakes in it yet.
quotes.author: L.M. Montgomery

quotes.text: We accept the love we think we deserve.
quotes.author: Stephen Chbosky

quotes.text: All we have to decide is what to do with the time that is given us.
quotes.author: J.R.R. Tolkien

quotes.text: The world is full of magic things, patiently waiting for our senses to grow sharper.
quotes.author: W.B. Yeats

quotes.text: And, when you want something, all the universe conspires in helping you to achieve it.
quotes.author: Paulo Coelho

quotes.text: One cannot think well, love well, sleep well, if one has not dined well.
quotes.author: Virginia Woolf

quotes.text: Happiness can be found even in the darkest of times, if one only remembers to turn on the light.
quotes.author: J.K. Rowling

quotes.text: To live is the rarest thing in the world. Most people exist, that is all.
quotes.author: Oscar Wilde

quotes.text: The marks humans leave are too often scars.
quotes.author: John Green

quotes.text: We need never be hopeless because we can never be irreparably broken.
quotes.author: John Green

quotes.text: There are far, far better things ahead than any we leave behind.
quotes.author: C.S. Lewis

quotes.text: Gratitude turns what we have into enough.
quotes.author: Melody Beattie

quotes.text: Enjoy the little things, for one day you may look back and realize they were the big things.
quotes.author: Robert Brault

quotes.text: Gratitude is not only the greatest of virtues, but the parent of all the others.
quotes.author: Cicero

quotes.text: When I started counting my blessings, my whole life turned around.
quotes.author: Willie Nelson

quotes.text: Gratitude makes sense of our past, brings peace for today, and creates a vision for tomorrow.
quotes.author: Melody Beattie

quotes.text: The more grateful I am, the more beauty I see.
quotes.author: Mary Davis

quotes.text: Happiness is not having what you want. It is appreciating what you have.
quotes.author: Chinese Proverb

quotes.text: Let us be grateful to people who make us happy; they are the charming gardeners who make our souls blossom.
quotes.author: Marcel Proust

quotes.text: Feeling gratitude and not expressing it is like wrapping a present and not giving it.
quotes.author: William Arthur Ward

quotes.text: If you are really thankful, what do you do? You share.
quotes.author: W. Clement Stone

quotes.text: There are only two ways to live your life. One is as though nothing is a miracle. The other is as though everything is a miracle.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: Gratitude is the fairest blossom which springs from the soul.
quotes.author: Henry Ward Beecher

quotes.text: The roots of all goodness lie in the soil of appreciation for goodness.
quotes.author: Dalai Lama

quotes.text: Silent gratitude isn't much use to anyone.
quotes.author: Gertrude Stein

quotes.text: Be thankful for what you have; you'll end up having more.
quotes.author: Oprah Winfrey

quotes.text: We often take for granted the very things that most deserve our gratitude.
quotes.author: Cynthia Ozick

quotes.text: Gratitude is the sign of noble souls.
quotes.author: Aesop

quotes.text: Acknowledging the good that you already have in your life is the foundation for all abundance.
quotes.author: Eckhart Tolle

quotes.text: Enough is a feast.
quotes.author: Buddhist Proverb

quotes.text: What if you gave someone a gift, and they neglected to thank you for it? Happens to God all the time.
quotes.author: Irish Proverb

quotes.text: Life is either a daring adventure or nothing at all.
quotes.author: Helen Keller

quotes.text: Twenty years from now you will be more disappointed by the things you didn't do than by the ones you did do.
quotes.author: Mark Twain

quotes.text: To live would be an awfully big adventure.
quotes.author: J.M. Barrie

quotes.text: Adventure is worthwhile in itself.
quotes.author: Amelia Earhart

quotes.text: The biggest adventure you can take is to live the life of your dreams.
quotes.author: Oprah Winfrey

quotes.text: Jobs fill your pocket, but adventures fill your soul.
quotes.author: Jamie Lyn Beatty

quotes.text: Man cannot discover new oceans unless he has the courage to lose sight of the shore.
quotes.author: Andre Gide

quotes.text: Life begins at the end of your comfort zone.
quotes.author: Neale Donald Walsch

quotes.text: Travel is fatal to prejudice, bigotry, and narrow-mindedness.
quotes.author: Mark Twain

quotes.text: It is not the mountain we conquer, but ourselves.
quotes.author: Sir Edmund Hillary

quotes.text: The world is a book and those who do not travel read only one page.
quotes.author: Saint Augustine

quotes.text: If you never go, you'll never know.
quotes.author: Travel Proverb

quotes.text: Take only memories, leave only footprints.
quotes.author: Chief Seattle

quotes.text: Not until we are lost do we begin to understand ourselves.
quotes.author: Henry David Thoreau

quotes.text: Difficult roads often lead to beautiful destinations.
quotes.author: Zig Ziglar

quotes.text: A ship in harbor is safe, but that is not what ships are built for.
quotes.author: John A. Shedd

quotes.text: Only those who will risk going too far can possibly find out how far one can go.
quotes.author: T.S. Eliot

quotes.text: Do not follow where the path may lead. Go instead where there is no path and leave a trail.
quotes.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quotes.text: Collect moments, not things.
quotes.author: Paulo Coelho

quotes.text: Once a year, go someplace you've never been before.
quotes.author: Dalai Lama
